evals.prompt: Build an ETL pipeline that imports product data from a third-party REST API into our PostgreSQL database. The API returns paginated JSON. Need: extraction with rate limiting, Pydantic validation, upsert logic (create or update by SKU), and error reporting.
evals.expected_output: ETL pipeline with API extraction, Pydantic models, upsert logic, and error handling

assertions.name: api_extraction
assertions.description: Fetches paginated data from REST API with rate limiting

assertions.name: pydantic_validation
assertions.description: Validates each record with Pydantic models

assertions.name: upsert_logic
assertions.description: Creates new records or updates existing by SKU

assertions.name: error_reporting
assertions.description: Collects and reports validation/processing errors per record

assertions.name: idempotent
assertions.description: Pipeline can be re-run safely without duplicating data

evals.prompt: Create a Django management command that processes a CSV file with 500K order records. It should: stream the file (not load all into memory), validate each row, batch-insert valid rows (1000 at a time), and generate an error report.
evals.expected_output: Streaming CSV processor with batch inserts, validation, and error report

assertions.name: streaming_read
assertions.description: Uses csv.reader or chunked reading, not loading entire file

assertions.name: batch_inserts
assertions.description: Uses bulk_create with batch_size for efficiency

assertions.name: validation
assertions.description: Each row validated before insertion

assertions.name: error_report
assertions.description: Invalid rows collected with row numbers and error details

assertions.name: memory_efficient
assertions.description: Constant memory usage regardless of file size

evals.prompt: Build an async data pipeline using Celery that: 1) fetches data from 3 different APIs in parallel, 2) transforms and merges the results, 3) stores in PostgreSQL, 4) sends a summary email. Use Celery chord for parallel fetch.
evals.expected_output: Celery task chain with chord for parallel fetch, transform, store, and notify tasks

assertions.name: celery_chord
assertions.description: Uses chord or group for parallel API fetching

assertions.name: three_fetchers
assertions.description: Separate tasks for each of the 3 API sources

assertions.name: transform_merge
assertions.description: Merge/transform task combines results from all 3 sources

assertions.name: database_store
assertions.description: Transformed data stored in PostgreSQL

assertions.name: email_notification
assertions.description: Summary email sent after pipeline completes

evals.prompt: Create a real-time event processing pipeline. Events come from a Kafka topic, need to be validated, enriched with user data from PostgreSQL, and forwarded to Elasticsearch for search indexing.
evals.expected_output: Event consumer with validation, DB enrichment, and Elasticsearch indexing

assertions.name: kafka_consumer
assertions.description: Consumes events from Kafka topic

assertions.name: event_validation
assertions.description: Validates event schema before processing

assertions.name: enrichment
assertions.description: Looks up user data from PostgreSQL to enrich events

assertions.name: elasticsearch_indexing
assertions.description: Indexes enriched events into Elasticsearch

assertions.name: error_handling
assertions.description: Dead letter queue or error logging for failed events

evals.prompt: Build a data pipeline that syncs users from our PostgreSQL database to a third-party CRM (HubSpot) via their API. Need: incremental sync (only changed records), conflict resolution, and retry on API failures.
evals.expected_output: Incremental sync pipeline with change detection, CRM API calls, and retry logic

assertions.name: incremental_sync
assertions.description: Only syncs records changed since last sync (using updated_at or CDC)

assertions.name: crm_api_calls
assertions.description: Creates/updates contacts in HubSpot API

assertions.name: conflict_resolution
assertions.description: Handles conflicts when records changed on both sides

assertions.name: retry_logic
assertions.description: Retries failed API calls with exponential backoff

assertions.name: sync_state
assertions.description: Tracks last sync timestamp for next run
